examples.title: Incremental Development Process
examples.description: General algorithm showing how increments are developed and delivered
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IncrementalDevelopment
BEGIN
 SET complete_requirements = GatherInitialRequirements()
 SET prioritized_features = Prioritize(complete_requirements)
 SET remaining_features = prioritized_features
 
 WHILE remaining_features NOT empty DO
 SET current_increment = GetNextIncrement(remaining_features)
 
 // Requirements Analysis for this increment
 SET increment_requirements = AnalyzeRequirements(current_increment)
 
 // Design Phase
 SET increment_design = Design(increment_requirements)
 
 // Implementation Phase
 SET increment_code = Implement(increment_design)
 
 // Testing Phase
 SET increment_test_results = Test(increment_code)
 
 IF TestPasses(increment_test_results) THEN
 // Deployment
 DeliverIncrement(increment_code)
 
 // Get Feedback
 SET user_feedback = GetUserFeedback()
 
 // Update requirements based on feedback
 UpdateRequirements(remaining_features, user_feedback)
 ELSE
 FixIssuesAndRetest()
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN CompleteSystem
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the cyclic nature of incremental development where each iteration analyzes requirements, designs, implements, tests, and deploys an increment while incorporating user feedback for future increments.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × m) where n=number of increments, m=features per increment
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) for storing increments and feedback

examples.title: RAD Model Development Process
examples.description: Algorithm for Rapid Application Development with emphasis on short cycles and prototyping
examples.code:
PROCEDURE RADModel
BEGIN
 SET time_frame = DefineTimeFrame() // Typically 60-90 days
 SET business_objectives = DefineObjectives()
 
 // Phase 1: Business Modeling
 SET business_relationships = ModelBusinessProcesses(business_objectives)
 
 // Phase 2: Data Modeling
 SET data_objects = IdentifyDataRequirements(business_relationships)
 
 // Phase 3: Process Modeling
 SET processes = DefineDataFlow(business_relationships, data_objects)
 
 // Phase 4: Application Generation
 SET prototype = BuildRapidPrototype(processes, data_objects)
 
 // Phase 5: Testing and Turnover
 WHILE prototype NOT fully validated DO
 SET user_feedback = PresentToUsers(prototype)
 SET modifications = IdentifyChanges(user_feedback)
 
 IF modifications within time_frame THEN
 SET prototype = ModifyPrototype(prototype, modifications)
 ELSE
 BREAK // Move to next iteration
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 SET final_system = CompleteSystem(prototype)
 RETURN final_system
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The RAD model emphasizes building functional prototypes quickly within tight timeframes (60-90 days), using reusable components, and incorporating user feedback rapidly to refine the application.
examples.timeComplexity: O(t) where t = number of prototype iterations within time frame

examples.title: Increment Planning and Prioritization
examples.description: Algorithm for planning and prioritizing features for each increment
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PlanNextIncrement
INPUT: all_features, completed_features, project_constraints
BEGIN
 SET available_capacity = CalculateCapacity(project_constraints)
 SET candidate_features = Subtract(all_features, completed_features)
 
 // Priority-based selection
 SET prioritized_list = SortByPriority(candidate_features)
 SET selected_features = []
 SET current_size = 0
 
 FOR EACH feature IN prioritized_list DO
 SET feature_size = EstimateSize(feature)
 
 IF current_size + feature_size <= available_capacity THEN
 Add feature TO selected_features
 current_size = current_size + feature_size
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check for dependencies
 SET resolved_features = ResolveDependencies(selected_features)
 
 RETURN resolved_features
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE CalculateCapacity
INPUT: resources, time_available, team_velocity
BEGIN
 SET capacity = (resources × time_available × team_velocity)
 RETURN capacity
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps plan each increment by selecting features based on priority, estimated size, and available project capacity, ensuring each increment is manageable and delivers maximum value.
examples.timeComplexity: O(f log f) where f = number of features to prioritize
